title: Concept Graph (Semantic)
description: Typed-edge concept graph emitted alongside concept_graph.json. Edges carry a type (prerequisite | is-a | related-to) and per-edge provenance. See docs/concept-graph/typed-edges.md for rule descriptions and precedence.

rule_versions.description: Map of rule name -> version integer for the rules that fired in this run.

run_id.description: Pipeline run that produced this node (REC-PRV-01, Worker P Wave 4.1). Sourced from the active DecisionCapture instance's run_id. Always populated on new runs; legacy nodes without this field still validate.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the node was emitted (REC-PRV-01, Worker P Wave 4.1). Equal to the artifact-level generated_at for a given run. Always populated on new runs; legacy nodes without this field still validate.

course_id.description: When TRAINFORGE_SCOPE_CONCEPT_IDS=true, concept ID format becomes '{course_id}:{slug}' and this field carries the scoping course_id. Default behavior: concept IDs are flat slugs, course_id absent.

occurrences.description: Chunk IDs that reference this concept (REC-LNK-01, Worker S Wave 5.1). Populated from chunk->concept inverted index at graph-build time. Sorted for deterministic output. Stable across re-chunks only when TRAINFORGE_CONTENT_HASH_IDS=true (Worker N's flag); position-based chunk IDs invalidate entries on re-chunk. Legacy nodes without this field still validate (optional).

source_refs.description: Wave 10: optional array of DART/Courseforge source references for this concept. Populated at graph-build time by copying source.source_references[] from occurrences[0] (the first chunk by sorted-ID ordering from Wave 5). Absence = 'unknown' (pre-Wave-9 corpora, or a concept whose first occurrence chunk carries no source refs). Lets consumers answer 'what source(s) introduce this concept' without a two-hop chunk dereference.

edges.$comment: REC-LNK-04 (Wave 5.2, Worker U): edges may cross node-type namespaces (federation-by-convention). Legacy taxonomic edges (is-a, prerequisite, related-to) connect concept node IDs. New pedagogical edge types reference heterogeneous endpoints by ID namespace: concept IDs (flat slug or '{course_id}:{slug}'), LO IDs (e.g. 'TO-NN', 'CO-NN', lowercased), chunk IDs (raw chunk.id strings), misconception IDs ('mc_[0-9a-f]{16}' per schemas/knowledge/misconception.schema.json), and question IDs. Consumers resolve endpoints by ID-namespace prefix; no new node types are added to the concept-graph schema itself.

provenance.$comment: REC-PRV-02 (Wave 6, Worker W): per-rule evidence sub-schemas with a oneOf discriminator keyed by the `rule` field. Each specific arm binds rule={rule_name} to evidence={matching $def}. The final FallbackProvenance arm matches any rule that isn't one of the 9 known rules (and allows evidence to be absent or any shape) — preserving backward-compat with legacy graphs and future rules. The arms are mutually exclusive by virtue of the `rule` const, so oneOf validation is unambiguous. Strict validation is opt-in via TRAINFORGE_STRICT_EVIDENCE=true (see lib/validators/evidence.py::get_schema): strip the fallback arm before validating. Each specific evidence $def sets additionalProperties:false to enforce shape when in strict mode; lenient mode passes through the fallback arm if a known-rule edge's evidence shape drifts. Wave 66 adds the 9th known rule: targets_concept_from_lo.

run_id.description: Pipeline run that produced this edge (REC-PRV-01, Worker P Wave 4.1). Sourced from the active DecisionCapture instance's run_id. Always populated on new runs; legacy edges without this field still validate.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the edge was emitted (REC-PRV-01, Worker P Wave 4.1). Equal to the artifact-level generated_at for a given run. Always populated on new runs; legacy edges without this field still validate.

IsAEvidence.description: Evidence emitted by rule is_a_from_key_terms (Trainforge/rag/inference_rules/is_a_from_key_terms.py). Captures which chunk supplied the definition, the child term, a trimmed excerpt of the definition, and the regex pattern that matched. Wave 11 adds optional source_references[] (chunk-anchored provenance copied from the chunk's source.source_references[] when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true).

source_references.description: Wave 11: optional array of DART/Courseforge source references copied from the originating chunk's source.source_references[]. Populated only when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true; absent on legacy corpora and when the source chunk carries no refs. Schema admits the field unconditionally so strict validators don't change behavior based on the flag.

PrerequisiteEvidence.description: Evidence emitted by rule prerequisite_from_lo_order (Trainforge/rag/inference_rules/prerequisite_from_lo_order.py). Records the first-LO skew that justified the directed prerequisite: the (source, target) concept pair's earliest LO id + positional index in course.learning_outcomes.

RelatedEvidence.description: Evidence emitted by rule related_from_cooccurrence (Trainforge/rag/inference_rules/related_from_cooccurrence.py). Records the co-occurrence weight that cleared the configurable threshold.

AssessesEvidence.description: Evidence emitted by rule assesses_from_question_lo (Trainforge/rag/inference_rules/assesses_from_question_lo.py). Materializes the explicit question->LO reference; optional source_chunk_id when the question carries a chunk pointer. Wave 11 adds optional source_references[] (copied from the referenced chunk's source.source_references[] when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true and source_chunk_id resolves to a chunk).

source_references.description: Wave 11: optional array of DART/Courseforge source references copied from the referenced chunk's source.source_references[]. Populated only when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true, source_chunk_id is set, and the resolved chunk carries refs.

ExemplifiesEvidence.description: Evidence emitted by rule exemplifies_from_example_chunks (Trainforge/rag/inference_rules/exemplifies_from_example_chunks.py). content_type discriminates which signal fired: 'content_type_label' (JSON-LD / data-cf-content-type) or 'chunk_type' (structural classification). Wave 11 adds optional source_references[] (copied from the example chunk's source.source_references[] when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true).

source_references.description: Wave 11: optional array of DART/Courseforge source references copied from the originating chunk's source.source_references[]. Populated only when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true.

MisconceptionOfEvidence.description: Evidence emitted by rule misconception_of_from_misconception_ref (Trainforge/rag/inference_rules/misconception_of_from_misconception_ref.py). Materializes the explicit misconception->concept pointer from the first-class Misconception entity.

DerivedFromObjectiveEvidence.description: Evidence emitted by rule derived_from_lo_ref (Trainforge/rag/inference_rules/derived_from_lo_ref.py). Materializes the chunk->LO reference that already exists on the chunk via learning_outcome_refs. Wave 11 adds optional source_references[] (copied from the chunk's source.source_references[] when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true).

source_references.description: Wave 11: optional array of DART/Courseforge source references copied from the originating chunk's source.source_references[]. Populated only when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true.

DefinedByEvidence.description: Evidence emitted by rule defined_by_from_first_mention (Trainforge/rag/inference_rules/defined_by_from_first_mention.py). first_mention_position is always 0 (the first entry after sort-ASC on occurrences[]) — emitted for forward-compat in case the rule later supports non-first picks. Wave 11 adds optional source_references[] (copied from the first-mention chunk's source.source_references[] when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true).

source_references.description: Wave 11: optional array of DART/Courseforge source references copied from the first-mention chunk's source.source_references[]. Populated only when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true and the chunks list is available for lookup.

IsAProvenance.description: Provenance arm for edges emitted by rule is_a_from_key_terms. Binds rule const + versioned evidence shape.

PrerequisiteProvenance.description: Provenance arm for edges emitted by rule prerequisite_from_lo_order.

RelatedProvenance.description: Provenance arm for edges emitted by rule related_from_cooccurrence.

AssessesProvenance.description: Provenance arm for edges emitted by rule assesses_from_question_lo.

ExemplifiesProvenance.description: Provenance arm for edges emitted by rule exemplifies_from_example_chunks.

MisconceptionOfProvenance.description: Provenance arm for edges emitted by rule misconception_of_from_misconception_ref.

DerivedFromObjectiveProvenance.description: Provenance arm for edges emitted by rule derived_from_lo_ref.

DefinedByProvenance.description: Provenance arm for edges emitted by rule defined_by_from_first_mention.

TargetsConceptEvidence.description: Wave 66: Evidence emitted by rule targets_concept_from_lo (Trainforge/rag/inference_rules/targets_concept_from_lo.py). Materializes the Wave 57 LO→concept edge from Courseforge JSON-LD learningObjectives[].targetedConcepts[]: each entry there becomes one typed edge carrying the bloom_level the LO targets the concept at. Required: lo_id (canonical LO identifier, e.g., 'TO-01' / 'co-05' — case-insensitive per Trainforge normalization), concept_id (concept slug, 'framework' / 'ecosystem-flow'), bloom_level (one of the six canonical Bloom levels). Optional source_references[] when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true.

bloom_level.$comment: Inlined canonical BloomLevel enum (matches schemas/taxonomies/bloom_verbs.json $defs.BloomLevel) so schema validators don't need to fetch the external taxonomy ref. Drift guard: Wave 48's schema-sourced cognitive-domain loader + Wave 55's canonical detect_bloom_level keep the Python constants in sync with this enum; a Courseforge / Trainforge regression test would catch divergence before it shipped.

source_references.description: Wave 66: optional source references copied from the emitting LO's page-level sourceReferences[] when TRAINFORGE_SOURCE_PROVENANCE=true.

TargetsConceptProvenance.description: Wave 66: Provenance arm for edges emitted by rule targets_concept_from_lo.

FallbackProvenance.description: Lenient fallback arm of the provenance oneOf. Matches any edge whose rule is NOT one of the 9 modeled rules (mutually exclusive by the `not:enum` discriminator). Evidence may be absent or any shape — preserving backward-compat with legacy graphs and with future rules not yet modeled as a specific $def. Strip this arm from the oneOf when strict validation is required (see lib/validators/evidence.py::get_schema(strict=True) or TRAINFORGE_STRICT_EVIDENCE=true). The strict-stripped schema then rejects any edge whose rule is unknown OR whose evidence shape drifts from its modeled $def. Wave 66 added the 9th modeled rule: targets_concept_from_lo.
